# Complete temporal-pyramid aggregation before runtime enablement

## Context

PR #14897 delivers the disabled-by-default scaffold for #14434: the pure aggregation engine, the IO service, the daemon entry point, and all six named source bindings. The live PR body still records unfinished runtime contracts and keeps `aggregationEnabled` false. To preserve that converged work while ending repeated review cycles, this successor owns every remaining enablement obligation in one coherent lane.

This is not a micro-ticket split. It is the completion lane after the scaffold merges, owned by `@neo-gpt`.

## The Problem

The scaffold is safe to merge while runtime-disabled, but enablement would currently outrun the contract:

- ADR 0028 requires both durable L1 session and L2 daily records; the branch proves the L2 engine but not the complete L1/L2 runtime path.
- `sandboxesGraduated` cannot use Discussion close time or marker-like prose as the fact source. The canonical author-action marker and its event timestamp must be read from the complete Discussion conversation.
- The lane must participate in the existing supervised orchestrator/backpressure topology rather than creating a second independent scheduler.
- `SUMMARY_DAILY` writes, bounded retention/version progression, and live durable-write evidence remain absent.
- The opt-in must stay false until the complete path is proven.

## The Architectural Reality

The governing surfaces were re-verified before filing:

- ADR 0028 §2.1/§2.3/§2.4/§2.6 fixes durable L1/L2, one `temporal-summary` collection, named metric sources, deterministic `SUMMARY_DAILY` writes, and unified plus per-agent partitions.
- ADR 0028 §4 assigns bounded retention/versioning to this implementation lane.
- ADR 0022 and #14434 require the aggregation work to share the existing orchestrator fairness and heavy-maintenance backpressure substrate.
- The scaffold lives in `ai/daemons/temporal-summary/{daemon,TemporalSummaryAggregationService}.mjs` and `ai/services/memory-core/helpers/temporalSummaryAggregationEngine.mjs`; the orchestrator owner surfaces are `ai/daemons/orchestrator/scheduling/{registry,pipeline}.mjs` and `ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/MaintenanceBackpressureService.mjs`.
- ADR 0024 already registers `SUMMARY_SESSION` and `SUMMARY_DAILY` as deterministic aggregation-lane node types.

## The Fix

Build on the merged scaffold and finish the lane in one PR:

1. Register a supervised, one-shot temporal-summary task in the existing orchestrator topology; keep cadence and heavy-lane fairness there rather than inside an independent child poller.
2. Materialize both L1 and L2 records for unified and per-agent partitions through the existing schema and pure engine.
3. Source `sandboxesGraduated` from exact `[GRADUATED_TO_TICKET: #N]` author actions with their event timestamps; paginate the full conversation and fail closed when evidence is unavailable.
4. Emit `SUMMARY_DAILY` only from this deterministic writer.
5. Define and test append-only version progression plus bounded retention.
6. Prove the real durable write and idempotent replay before runtime enablement.

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `AiConfig.temporalSummary.aggregationEnabled` | ADR 0019 + #14897 scaffold contract | Remains `false` until every AC below passes | Disabled; no partial writes | Config JSDoc + PR body | Disabled-path spec and live boot |
| Temporal-summary scheduled task | ADR 0022 + #14434 | Existing orchestrator dispatches one supervised, backpressure-aware run | Defer without writing | Service/task JSDoc | Registry, fairness, lease, and failure-path specs |
| L1/L2 `temporal-summary` records | ADR 0028 §2.1/§2.3/§2.6 | Session + daily, unified + per-agent, idempotent per window/version | Fail closed; never invent a partition | Engine/service JSDoc | Focused unit proof + real Chroma upsert/replay |
| `sandboxesGraduated` | ADR 0028 §2.4 + canonical ideation graduation marker | Count exact author-action graduations by event time from the complete conversation | Unavailable evidence is not a numeric zero | Source-binding JSDoc | Pagination, prose-rejection, timestamp, and absence specs |
| `SUMMARY_DAILY` | ADR 0024 + ADR 0028 §2.3 | Deterministic lane is the sole writer | No label on write failure | Schema/producer JSDoc | Graph-write specs + extractor untouched |
| Version/retention policy | ADR 0028 §4 | Append-only version minting with bounded retained history | Runtime stays disabled | Policy JSDoc | Version-bump, idempotence, and retention specs |

## Decision Record impact

`aligned-with ADR 0028`; `aligned-with ADR 0022`; `aligned-with ADR 0024`. No ADR amendment is required unless implementation changes one of those fixed boundaries.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `aggregationEnabled` remains false until every other criterion is satisfied.
- [ ] The existing orchestrator owns cadence and dispatch; the temporal task is supervised, one-shot, bounded, and backpressure-aware.
- [ ] Durable L1 session and L2 daily records are written for unified and per-agent partitions without duplicate attribution.
- [ ] `sandboxesGraduated` accepts only exact author-action graduation markers, uses the marker event timestamp, paginates the full conversation, rejects marker-like prose, and fails closed when source evidence is unavailable.
- [ ] `SUMMARY_DAILY` is written only by this deterministic lane; the semantic extractor remains unchanged.
- [ ] Version bumps mint append-only records, replay is idempotent, and retention bounds growth.
- [ ] Focused tests cover success, deferral, pagination, partial-source failure, write failure, replay, and retention.
- [ ] Live validation proves one real Chroma upsert, one idempotent replay, and one orchestrator-owned execution before enablement.

## Out of Scope

- L3–L5 dynamic synthesis.
- UI or query consumers.
- Deep-history backfill.
- Direction breakdown from #14811.
- A second daemon, scheduler, collection, or extractor-prompt label path.

## Avoided Traps

- No micro-ticket decomposition of this completion lane.
- No prose-derived metric values.
- No Discussion-close-time proxy for graduation time.
- No independent `setInterval` scheduler beside the orchestrator.
- No enablement based on unit-only evidence.

- 2026-07-11T00:32:00Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #15016
- 2026-07-11T00:43:01Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `454a13a` - "feat(memory-core): sandboxesGraduated from exact marker author-actions by event time (#14938)

AC4: fetchSandboxesGraduated now counts only exact [GRADUATED_TO_TICKET: #N] (and Epic #N) author-action markers, window-filtered by each marker's EVENT timestamp — the enclosing comment's '### @author commented on <ISO>' boundary, or the discussion createdAt for a marker in the original post. It never uses closedAt: the graduation event is the author posting the marker, not the discussion close (which can happen much later, or never).

Rejects marker-like prose: a bare [GRADUATED_TO_TICKET] (predominant in the corpus, mostly backtick-wrapped mentions like 'run STEP_BACK before the marker') carries no ticket-naming payload, so it is not an author-action. Fails closed when a marker's event time cannot be resolved — never borrows the close time.

New extractGraduationActions helper parses the comment-boundary timestamps and matches the exact ticket-naming bracket. V-B-A'd against the live synced corpus: confirmed it preserves per-comment '### @author commented on <ISO>' headers, and that the bare-vs-ticket-naming split is the real action/prose discriminator.

Tests: rewrote the two sandboxesGraduated tests — event-time windowing (closedAt deliberately OUT of window to prove it is unused), prose rejection, Epic #N form, original-post createdAt stamping, and fail-closed on an unresolvable event time; 14/14 green."
- 2026-07-11T00:50:32Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `1de781b` - "feat(memory-core): write the SUMMARY_SESSION/SUMMARY_DAILY graph node (#14938)

AC5: persistTemporalRecord now writes the per-level durable graph node (SUMMARY_SESSION / SUMMARY_DAILY) via Memory_GraphService.upsertNode after the Chroma upsert — keyed by the same deterministic doc id (idempotent overwrite on both sides), with semanticVectorId linking the node back to the Chroma row. This deterministic lane is the SOLE writer of those labels; the semantic extractor never mints them.

Durable/dynamic boundary guard: only L1/L2 (durable) levels mint a node; a non-durable L3-L5 level writes none, so the boundary can never be breached with a persisted label.

ADR 0024 §2.2 already registers SUMMARY_SESSION/SUMMARY_DAILY bound to 'deterministic aggregation-lane writes only' (grep-verified) — this PR is precisely that write path, so no node-type amendment is required; the ADR 0028 §2.7 pre-declared obligation is satisfied.

Tests: the persist test asserts BOTH the Chroma upsert AND the SUMMARY_DAILY node (id / type / name / semanticVectorId / properties); a new test proves SUMMARY_SESSION for L1 and NO node for a non-durable (weekly) tier; 15/15 green. The spec harness now stubs GraphService.upsertNode by default so no unit test touches the real graph."
- 2026-07-11T01:09:46Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `daa4ebe` - "fix(orchestrator): temporal-summary is a supervised-child one-shot, not in-process (#14938)

Addresses two of the three draft-intake blockers @neo-gpt raised on PR #15016.

Blocker 2 (execution model): ADR 0028 §2.1 prescribes a SUPERVISED-CHILD scheduled task (the landed #12676 heavy-maintenance pattern), not in-process. I had over-corrected 'no independent setInterval poller' (correct) into 'in-process-async' (wrong axis). Fix: registry descriptor in-process-async -> supervised-child-process; a buildTaskDefinitions entry spawns a one-shot child (ai/scripts/maintenance/aggregate-temporal-summary.mjs) that runs exactly one runCycle() and exits — no timer, no self-reschedule, so still no independent scheduler beside the orchestrator; removed the in-process runTemporalSummaryTask runner + the injected orchestrator service field/import.

Blocker 1 (runtime crash): temporal-summary was in TASK_REGISTRY but absent from buildTaskDefinitions/createInitialTaskState, so markStarted('temporal-summary') set .running on undefined and threw. The taskDefinition entry now seeds the initial task-state, closing it. My earlier pipeline test stubbed taskStateService and masked exactly this — the same seam-stub blind spot as the staleAfterMs crash.

The orchestrator still owns cadence (getDueTask + intervals/enables) and the heavy lease: executeSupervisedCandidate -> executeWithMaintenance -> acquireLeaseAndExecute spawns the child under the lease. The child holds no lease of its own.

Tests: registry descriptor test -> supervised-child-process; pipeline dispatch test -> supervised (processSupervisorService.runTask spawn), wiring test drops the injected service; registry.spec + pipeline.spec 40/40 green.

Blocker 3 (ADR 0014 §9 deployment classification -> local-only) follows next."
- 2026-07-11T01:09:46Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `f9dfe94` - "fix(orchestrator): classify temporal-summary local-only per ADR 0014 §9 (#14938)

Addresses the third draft-intake blocker @neo-gpt raised on PR #15016.

The aggregation folds checkout-bound sources (repo-tracked resources/content, 'git log --first-parent origin/dev', learn/agentos/decisions), so it is local-only: a cloud tenant deployment has neither the Neo-maintainer checkout nor origin/dev, and gets its corpus via push-ingest, not this local scan.

ADR 0014 §9 (the new-Orchestrator-lane re-review trigger): amended the taxonomy with a dated temporal-summary classification (local-only), citing §9 and mirroring the tenant-repo-sync amendment shape; updated the local-only set.

Config: added orchestrator.localOnly.temporalSummaryEnabled (NEO_ORCHESTRATOR_TEMPORAL_SUMMARY_ENABLED) to the tracked template. (config.mjs is the gitignored operator snapshot; the resolver's missing-key fallback resolves it to the deployment-profile default until re-materialized.)

Getter: temporalSummaryEnabled now ANDs the deployment gate resolveDeploymentEnabled('temporalSummaryEnabled') with the ADR 0028 opt-in AiConfig.temporalSummary.aggregationEnabled, both read at the use site (ADR 0019). The lane runs only in a local profile that has explicitly opted in; a cloud profile disables it by default.

The getter composes already-tested primitives; a dedicated getter unit test is impractical given the pre-existing Orchestrator.spec construction issue in this worktree."
- 2026-07-11T01:16:43Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `4682309` - "feat(memory-core): append-only contract-version + bounded-retention engine policy (#14938)

AC6 (engine foundation). TEMPORAL_AGGREGATION_VERSION is the aggregation CONTRACT version stamped on every record (composeUnifiedRecord/composeAgentRecord default to it). Re-folding a window under the same contract keeps the version -> stable deterministic doc id -> idempotent overwrite (the settling-sources case, run every cycle). A material fold/source-contract change bumps the constant -> new records mint under the next version while the prior version's records stay queryable -> append-only history. This is NOT a per-cycle counter, so version history does not explode.

versionsToPrune is the pure retention decision: keep the newest DEFAULT_RETAINED_VERSIONS (3) per window+track and return the older overflow to delete. Retention is defined by COUNT (newest-N), so a sparse/gapped version sequence still bounds cleanly; non-integer / <1 versions are ignored.

ADR 0028 §4 delegates the retention/versioning policy to this L1/L2 sub; this is that policy, within the version metadata field the ADR fixes. The service applies the prune (read existing versions -> delete the overflow) in the next increment; the version default is already wired through the compose paths.

Tests: version default + append-only id progression + versionsToPrune (keep/prune, sparse+dupe+unordered, invalid-ignored, coercion, default-3); 28/28 green."
- 2026-07-11T01:21:46Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `51e60d3` - "feat(memory-core): service applies bounded-version retention prune (#14938)

AC6 (service application — completes AC6). persistTemporalRecord now calls pruneOldVersions after the Chroma upsert + graph write. For a window+track whose contract version has passed the retained bound, it queries the persisted versions, keeps the newest DEFAULT_RETAINED_VERSIONS, and deletes the older overflow from BOTH stores — the Chroma docs (collection.delete) and the SUMMARY_* graph nodes (GraphService.removeNodes) — by the same doc ids.

Guarded: version <= retained -> overflow impossible -> the query is skipped entirely, so steady-state re-folds (contract version unchanged) pay zero prune overhead. The prune activates only after a material contract bump.

With the prior engine-foundation commit, AC6 is complete: append-only contract-version progression + bounded retention, keeping durable-tier growth bounded per ADR 0028 §4.

Tests: prune deletes the overflow from both stores (5 versions, retained 3 -> prune {v2,v1}); a no-op that never queries at the steady-state version; 17/17 green."
- 2026-07-11T01:29:33Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `88b7ad6` - "fix(memory-core): AC4 counts only marker-leading graduation actions, deduped, fail-closed (#14938)

Addresses @neo-gpt's AC4 over-count audit on PR #15016.

The prior extractGraduationActions matched the ticket-bearing token anywhere and emitted every occurrence — a marker in prose, or a later quote of a real graduation, double-counted (his probe: one real action + an original-post prose example + a later quote returned 3 rows instead of 1). That violated both 'reject marker-like prose' and sandboxesGraduated cardinality.

Fix: parse the author-ACTION LINE shape — the marker must LEAD its line after only allowed wrappers (heading #s, bold, backticks), matching how graduations are actually posted in the corpus (heading-led and bold+backtick-wrapped). Inline prose, blockquotes, and fenced/indented code are rejected. Dedupe per discussion+ticket on the earliest evidenced action, so a quote of the same graduation never re-counts.

Timestamp fail-closed: a marker outside a dated comment (e.g. the original post) has no event timestamp — the discussion creation time is not the marker's edit time — so it is uncounted rather than proxied. V-B-A'd against the corpus: the two real ticketed markers are both in dated comments, so this loses no observed facts.

Tests: rewrote the fixtures to realistic marker-leading lines + the falsifiers (inline prose, quoted/repeated marker, original-post fail-closed, out-of-window, heading and bold/backtick wrappers); 17/17 green."
- 2026-07-11T01:31:45Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `f83e098` - "test(memory-core): AC7 partial-source-failure fails the window closed (#14938)

Closes one of the two AC7 matrix gaps @neo-gpt flagged. When one of the six source fetches throws, fetchWindowSources rejects, so the cycle aborts rather than folding a record missing a source (which would silently undercount a durable metric). The reject propagates through runCycle -> the supervised child exits non-zero -> no half-written window is persisted.

The other named gap, deferral, is covered at the orchestrator/backpressure layer (executeWithMaintenance defers a heavy task under a held lease); success / replay / retention / pagination were already covered. Service spec 18/18."
- 2026-07-11T01:35:59Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `3a06411` - "fix(memory-core): retention prune is graph-first + retry-safe; restore removeNodes stub (#14938)

Addresses @neo-gpt's 3rd draft-audit findings on the AC6 retention path.

Delete ordering: reversed to graph-first, THEN Chroma. The Chroma doc is the 'still needs pruning' signal, so deleting it LAST keeps the prune retry-safe — any failure before the Chroma delete leaves the version visible to re-prune next cycle, never a Chroma-gone / graph-orphaned record. The prior order (Chroma first) meant a graph-delete failure permanently orphaned the node (Chroma gone -> the next prune's query never sees that version again).

Stub leak: the prune test set GraphService.removeNodes without restoring it, leaking the stub to other tests. Captured the original in beforeAll and restored it in afterAll alongside upsertNode, with a default no-op so no test hits the real graph.

Tests: added the partial-failure/retry witness — the graph node is deleted BEFORE the Chroma doc, and a Chroma-delete throw rejects (retryable) with order ['graph','chroma']; 19/19 green."
- 2026-07-11T01:53:19Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `6c1ba78` - "test(orchestrator): pin temporal-summary in 3 cross-spec contract expectations (#14938)

Addresses the 3 PR-caused contract-pin failures @neo-gpt found in the hosted unit run (job 86497267378), each deterministic (all retries failed) — spec files elsewhere pin the exact shapes this PR extends:

1. config.template.spec — Config.orchestrator.localOnly expected now includes the additive temporalSummaryEnabled: null leaf.
2. Orchestrator.spec — the persisted-state task-key expectation includes temporal-summary (between graphlog-compaction and chromaDefrag, matching buildTaskDefinitions insertion order).
3. Orchestrator.spec — the DEFAULT_HEAVY_MAINTENANCE_TASK_NAMES pin includes temporal-summary (a SECOND copy of the heavy-set pin, beyond the MaintenanceBackpressureService.spec one already updated).

The focused five-file matrix caught the local pins; these three live in cross-cutting spec files the matrix did not run. Verified config.template.spec locally (13/13). Orchestrator.spec stack-overflows in this local worktree (a Base.mjs processConfigs recursion — a local-env artifact; the hosted CI run executes it fine, which is how these pins surfaced), so the two Orchestrator.spec fixes are contract-matched and rely on the exact-head CI re-run to green."
- 2026-07-11T10:11:34Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `9b7144a` - "fix(temporal-summary): half-open source windows + AC7 write-failure/replay + AC8 graph-readiness gate (#14938)

Addresses @neo-gpt's exact-head private-draft audit findings on this PR.

Half-open source windows (AC3 no-duplicate attribution): fetchDevCommits + fetchAdrsLanded relied on git log --since/--until, which are INCLUSIVE on both ends — a commit whose committer-date equals a window boundary landed in two adjacent L1/L2 windows, double-counting boundary commits/ADR adds and contradicting the half-open [start, end) contract the engine's window planners preserve. Both fetches now emit each commit's %cI and apply the authoritative half-open filter in JS (--since/--until stay only as a coarse scan bound). Two boundary-witness tests assert a commit/ADR add exactly at windowEnd is excluded.

AC7 production boundaries: added the two focused witnesses the AC names — (1) a rejected Chroma upsert aborts before any SUMMARY_* graph label is minted (the upsert is awaited before the durable graph write, so no orphan node points at an unwritten row); (2) persisting the same record twice re-upserts one deterministic id on both stores (idempotent replay, no duplicate).

AC8 preflight: the one-shot child now awaits GraphService.initAsync() before runCycle's first SUMMARY_* write, mirroring the sibling standalone graph consumers (upsertNode does not self-init).

JSDoc drift: class doc now says the Orchestrator dispatches this as a supervised one-shot child (not in-process); fetchSandboxesGraduated now says a marker outside a dated comment fails closed (not proxied to createdAt), matching the corrected implementation.

Verified: TemporalSummaryAggregationService.spec + temporalSummaryAggregationEngine.spec 51 passed locally; child parses + its GraphService import resolves."
- 2026-07-11T11:28:10Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `4b2a479` - "fix(temporal-summary): address review — ready() lifecycle, durable-boundary-first, reply/tilde parsing, doc sync (#14938)

Addresses @neo-gpt's CHANGES_REQUESTED review (each V-B-A'd against source before accepting).

RA2 [lifecycle]: the one-shot child awaits GraphService.ready(), not initAsync(). Base explicitly forbids awaiting initAsync() externally (it double-inits — the fresh-process probe showed calls:2 vs ready() calls:1); ready() awaits the framework-triggered init exactly once. Six sibling standalone scripts share the initAsync() anti-pattern — a separate follow-up, not this lane.

RA3 [durable boundary]: persistTemporalRecord resolves the level FIRST and no-ops a non-durable tier before ANY store write, so L3-L5 produce zero Chroma writes AND zero graph writes (was: unconditional Chroma upsert, then a graph-only durable guard). The weekly test now asserts zero Chroma upserts, not just zero nodes.

RA1b [AC4]: parse the reply boundary so a graduation marker inside a reply binds to the reply's own event time, not the parent comment's.

RA1c [AC4]: reject tilde-fenced markers exactly like backtick-fenced ones. A new falsifier covers both the reply-timestamp binding and the tilde fence.

RA4 [authority sync]: reconciled the version-policy prose that described a predecessor contract (temporalSummarySchema, the engine version params, and the ADR 0024 node-model section) to the implemented material contract-version policy — a same-version re-fold overwrites in place (idempotent replay), a material contract bump mints a new append-only version, retention bounded. config.template JSDoc corrected from daemon poll interval to orchestrator dispatch cadence.

Verified: service + engine + config.template specs 65 passed; AC8 re-validated with the exact listNodeRecordsByType durable-node count (not the LIMIT-50 searchNodes cap). RA1a addressed in the review reply — the sync cap is 100/100 (not 50/20) and full pagination is an upstream DiscussionService concern."

- 2026-07-11T11:37:48Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #15031
- 2026-07-11T12:17:32Z @neo-gpt cross-referenced by #15035
